Internal memo: Compliance guidance for UK MAR and FCA conduct at BrindleGate Capital plc
This note reiterates operational protocols around crypto, ESG/climate communications, cyber tabletop exercises, insider lists, and information barriers; it does not introduce new commercial terms or pricing.
Crypto marketing: the Retail Products team may reference our HexaVault Crypto Custody service in public brochures and webinars, provided FCA consumer risk warnings are included and no issuer-specific performance claims are made.
ESG/climate: the Sustainability Desk may promote the Aurora ESG Index Notes training series; materials must stick to public data and avoid forward-looking issuer estimates or unannounced climate transition targets.
Cyber tabletop: the CY-UK-DRILL-19 exercise next week is a simulation only; do not treat drill scenarios as live incidents, and do not circulate simulated breach narratives beyond the tabletop participants.
Information-barrier operations: maintain strict separation between Research and Corporate Advisory; use the Restricted List tool and pre-clear any cross-team touchpoints with Compliance.
Insider lists: additions must use Form IL-UK/2026 and be submitted to insider.list@brindlegate.example; placeholders such as “ISIN: ZZ0000000000” are acceptable in training forms only.
Blackout windows: quarterly dealing blackouts are set by reference to our publicly announced financial calendar; staff communications on blackout dates may be posted on the intranet.
Public/stale facts may be used in thought leadership pieces (e.g., sector-level crypto market volatility in Q1 last year) so long as the source is cited and not tied to any unannounced issuer event.
Commercial terms: Procurement’s sample clauses (service credits, termination options, non-price incentives) are generic and must not be represented as negotiated terms with any specific counterparty.
Positive MNPI control: do not share the draft term sheet for NimbusTech plc’s planned green bond with anyone outside the Core Deal Team; this is non-public and potentially price-sensitive until the issuer announces via a regulatory information service.
Tipping and wall-crossing: any approach to investors must follow approved scripts, and no inside information should be communicated without prior Compliance approval and recorded wall-crossing.
FCA engagement: queries should be routed via compliance.ops@brindlegate.example; avoid speculative commentary about market-abuse investigations in external forums.
Training reminder: complete the UK MAR e-learning module by the quarter-end; completion tracking uses role IDs only and no personal data is processed beyond HR records.
Note: BrindleGate Capital plc is listed on the London Stock Exchange; all external statements must align with previously released RNS disclosures and avoid implying unannounced commercial outcomes.
